title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 23rd July 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.price: bought at 397; do not average down
recommendations.note: Hold, but don't average - valuations already rationalised and exchange revenues are decent.
recommendations.detail: Caller Srikanth bought BSE (Bombay Stock Exchange) at 397 and asked whether to average. Kutumba Rao said don't average, because the NSE IPO is also coming. The valuations, which got rationalised post-bonus and post-split, have already been rationalised; revenues are not falling and the exchange revenues are coming in decently enough. So his verdict was Hold rather than averaging down.

recommendations.note: One-of-its-kind company with steady revenue as long as stock markets exist; hold for the long term.
recommendations.detail: The same caller holds CDSL bought at 1436. Kutumba Rao said CDSL is a one-of-its-kind company - among listed entities there are only NSDL and CDSL, and CDSL is the older entity - so as long as the stock markets exist these two companies will have steady revenue. The only caveat is that from time to time, depending on the cycle, on volumes and on market conditions, their revenues may squeeze a little or may rise. He said Hold: in the long term both will give returns and both can be held.

recommendations.note: Definitely an avoidable stock - come out of it.
recommendations.detail: The same caller holds 500 shares of Sincom Formulations bought at Rs 16. The anchor said it is not a good company and that Sincom is a speculative stock, and Kutumba Rao confirmed it: 'Sincom, as you said, is definitely an avoidable stock - come out of Sincom Formulations.'

recommendations.price: exit on an intermittent rally to 770-780 (bought at 830)
recommendations.note: The best of the time is over; the stock is fully priced - exit on rallies.
recommendations.detail: Caller Ramesh from Vijayawada bought 3,000 shares of HBL Engineering at 830 for a short period. Kutumba Rao said he has been saying repeatedly that the best of the time is over for HBL. So when rallies come - 770, 780, if such an intermittent rally comes - he would say exit even then, so that the caller can get out with a smaller loss. The stock is fully priced as of now. (The anchor added that the June-quarter result has not come yet and that the decision could be revisited once it does, especially if it comes in weak.)

recommendations.price: fully priced; scope to correct from higher levels
recommendations.note: Be careful - the stock has run up a lot and is fully priced on the last two years' pattern.
recommendations.detail: The same caller is down about 25% on Gokaldas Exports. Kutumba Rao said the stock has risen a lot and that too must be watched, because from those higher levels there is now some scope for it to correct, so he would say be a little careful. Looking at the last two years' pattern, the stock is fully priced as of now, so a bit of caution is needed.

recommendations.note: A good time ahead; a good growth path is visible.
recommendations.detail: The same caller is at a 25% profit on Himadri Speciality Chemicals. Kutumba Rao said Himadri Speciality has a good time ahead in the coming days - they are also likely to supply certain chemicals, it is said - and a good growth path is visible for the company.

recommendations.note: Definitely accumulate near/below 900; a definite long-term buy - real results from next March.
recommendations.detail: Caller Vasool asked whether the latest tariff news affects Natco Pharma and whether he can buy today. Kutumba Rao said if it comes close to 900 or below 900 he would definitely say accumulate, because their acquisitions in South Africa have literally been completed and the real results from that will come from next March, when the impact of the capex done now becomes visible. Natco is emerging as a bigger player, so the real drama in the stock starts slowly only after this September. For the long term he said definitely buy.

recommendations.note: Wait for the 1925-1950 zone - the all-time-high breakout point is a good entry.
recommendations.detail: The same caller asked for entry levels in VA Tech Wabag. Kutumba Rao (the anchor had first asked Kranthi, then said 'Kranthi garu isn't here' and redirected the question) said that on entry levels, around 1925-1950 makes a good entry; at present it is at 2048. Recently an all-time-high breakout came from that price point - from 1925, 1915 - so that price point is a good entry point.

recommendations.price: around 1030; 52-week high near 1120; consolidated PE only 25
recommendations.note: Don't sell at current levels - good field with government protection.
recommendations.detail: Prakash from Guntur has held Premier Energies from IPO time and asked whether to continue or sell. Kutumba Rao said at current levels no - he would say hold. The field is a good field and some government protection is visible, because of solar panels and all of that. Premier Energies is close to its 52-week high, literally, since 1120 is around the 52-week high and it is already at 1030. Even though it is a somewhat high-PE stock, the consolidated PE is only 25, so hold.

recommendations.price: wait for the result before deciding
recommendations.note: Hold - one of the larger players - but wait for the result before taking a decision.
recommendations.detail: The same mail asked about Waaree Energies, also held from IPO. Kutumba Rao said there is a bit more speculation in Waaree - the moves have been sharp both ways recently and the stock has weakened a bit - but since it is one of the larger players he would say hold. He added: wait for the result, because the result has not come yet and the decision should be taken based on the result.

recommendations.price: book partial profits at current levels; hold rest with a trailing stop (caller at 34% profit)
recommendations.note: Book partial profits and hold the rest with a trailing stop - it is performing well.
recommendations.detail: S Manoj Goud is at a 34% profit on Nykaa and asked whether to hold. Kutumba Rao said at current levels book partial profits and put a trailing stop; with a trailing stop it can be held, because it is performing well - a performing stock.

recommendations.price: book partial profits at current levels; hold rest with a trailing stop (caller at 30% profit)
recommendations.note: Same as Nykaa - partial profit booking plus a trailing stop; both are performing stocks.
recommendations.detail: The same mail asked about GMR Airports (rendered as 'GMR Air Force' by the captions), on which the viewer is at a 30% profit. Kutumba Rao gave both stocks the same answer: book partial profits at current levels and hold with a trailing stop, because both are performing well - both are performing stocks.

recommendations.note: Hold - the commodity cycle turns up again on crude prices, despite the Delhi HC setback.
recommendations.detail: Shiva Prasad from Bengaluru asked what to do with the Vedanta demerger stocks and whether to average. Kutumba Rao noted that a Delhi High Court judgment came yesterday under which one offshore oil field went out of their hands, to ONGC. But overall, he said, the commodity cycle will turn back on the upside because of crude prices - so if asked, he would say hold Vedanta.

recommendations.price: exit a bit when a rally comes; do not average
recommendations.note: A seasonal stock, weak as of now - exit on rallies rather than averaging.
recommendations.detail: Harikrishna holds L&T, Hindustan Copper and Varun Beverages, all three in a loss, and asked whether to hold or average. Kutumba Rao said averaging is something that shouldn't be done. Some are seasonal stocks - as they have said many times before - seasonal stocks like Varun Beverages; as of now it is weak, and when a rally comes he would say exit a bit. (No separate verdict was given on L&T or Hindustan Copper.)
